name: unity-editor
description: Remote control Unity Editor via CLI using unityctl. Use when working with Unity projects to launch/stop editor, enter/exit play mode, compile scripts, view logs, load scenes, run tests, capture screenshots, or execute C# code for debugging. Activate when user mentions Unity, play mode, compilation, or needs to interact with a running Unity Editor.
capabilities: Launch and stop Unity Editor from command line
Compile C# scripts and view compilation errors
Enter and exit play mode without using mouse
Execute arbitrary C# code in editor for debugging
author: DirtybitGames
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation-only file containing no executable code. All 64 static findings are false positives: bash command examples in SKILL.md were misidentified as shell execution, local screenshot capability was flagged as upload, status commands were labeled reconnaissance, and cryptographic patterns matched non-existent code. The skill merely describes how to use the external unityctl CLI tool. All network communication is localhost-only.
valueStatement: Stop switching between Unity Editor and terminal when developing games. This skill lets AI agents control Unity directly from the command line to compile scripts, enter play mode, capture screenshots, and execute debug code.
userTitle: Control Unity Editor from command line
limitations: Requires Unity Editor with unityctl plugin installed
Bridge daemon must be running before using commands
Only works with local Unity instances
C# script execution requires specific method signature

useCases.targetUser: Game developers using AI coding assistants
useCases.title: Rapid iteration on game scripts
useCases.description: Edit C# scripts in your AI chat, then compile and test immediately without leaving the conversation flow.

useCases.title: Debug without editor context switching
useCases.description: Execute debug code and inspect game objects from terminal while viewing logs in the same window.

useCases.title: Automate Unity editor workflows
useCases.description: Script entire Unity workflows including compilation, play mode testing, and screenshot capture for CI/CD pipelines.

promptTemplates.title: Compile and check errors
promptTemplates.prompt: I just modified some C# scripts in my Unity project. Can you compile them and show me any errors?

promptTemplates.title: Test in play mode
promptTemplates.prompt: Enter play mode, wait 5 seconds, capture a screenshot, then exit play mode

promptTemplates.title: Debug player position
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute C# code to find the GameObject named Player and return its current position

promptTemplates.title: Run tests and generate report
promptTemplates.prompt: Run all play mode tests and show me the results with any failures

bestPractices: Always run unityctl status first to verify bridge and editor are connected
Use unityctl asset refresh after modifying scripts before entering play mode
Write complex C# scripts to files and execute with -f flag for better debugging
antiPatterns: Do not execute untrusted C# code from unknown sources in your Unity editor
Avoid running multiple bridge instances for the same project
Do not rely on this for production builds - use Unity batch mode instead

faq.question: Which Unity versions are compatible?
faq.answer: Unity 6.0 and later versions with netstandard2.1 support. The tool auto-detects your Unity installation.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many commands I can run?
faq.answer: No hard limits, but the bridge maintains a 1000-entry log buffer. Commands timeout after 30 seconds by default.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, the CLI returns appropriate exit codes and supports JSON output format for automation scripts.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this tool?
faq.answer: All communication happens locally on your machine. The bridge only binds to localhost and does not expose remote access.

faq.question: What if the connection to Unity is lost?
faq.answer: The bridge automatically reconnects after Unity domain reloads. Use unityctl status to check connection health.

faq.question: How does this compare to Unity batch mode?
faq.answer: Unlike batch mode, this works while Unity Editor is open, providing visual feedback and interactive debugging.
